channel.title: Finally got my KS18 running smoothly on bare metal
channel.description:
On an HP Elitebook with 1TB 32GB RAM, running Fedora 43 Workstation, Whonix & Kicksecure all VMs
Just an applause to the KS Devs on a great OS

item.title: Finally got my KS18 running smoothly on bare metal
item.dc:creator: Patrick
item.description:
JustPassinThru:

I was reflecting on the comments made by GrapheneOS devs regarding KickSecure and wondered why the hate?

See: GrapheneOS Attacks Kicksecure! What should the response be?

Read full topic
item.source: Finally got my KS18 running smoothly on bare metal

item.title: Finally got my KS18 running smoothly on bare metal
item.dc:creator: JustPassinThru
item.description:
I was reflecting on the comments made by GrapheneOS devs regarding KickSecure and wondered why the hate? I have installed Luks2/Argon2id, hardened my VMs and am “relatively” safer side of internet actions. Mind you, i have been using exclusively Graphene since Micay left Copperhead and started Graphene. I see some say KS is nothing more than Debian on steroids..

I tried installing Qubes, and after a week or so of attempts, i stumbled across KickSecure (17 at the time) and tried it out. The upgrade from 17 to 18 was a game changer. Wayland, Virtio, etc,

The hardest part of KS was getting used to the no-sudo in user profile. But after a while, it becomes second nature. Bootup, Check Updates, install, reboot. I have to admit that i had to sneak a browser in to sysmaint for ease of setup. because the switching back and forth is basically a deal-breaker for usage. but as long as i am only using the browser for access for info from trusted sources, i deem it to be relatively safe

Read full topic
item.source: Finally got my KS18 running smoothly on bare metal
